channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida biologists capture heaviest panther ever recorded in the Sunshine State
item.description: Florida biologists captured the heaviest panther ever recorded in the state as part of a research program into the endangered animal's movements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 'Saturday Night Live' 50th anniversary celebration is finally here. Here's how to watch
item.description: Fifty seasons of “Saturday Night Live” sketches, songs and special guests will be celebrated Sunday as the variety show celebrates its landmark anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: U.S. states seem to mark Presidents Day with as many different names and honors as there have been presidents.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, KTLA 5

item.title: Missouri community supports Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: Wyoming authorities have entered a highway tunnel to begin picking through the wreckage of a fiery multivehicle crash a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Flooding takes toll on southern, eastern Kentucky: Here's what officials are seeing
item.description: From Bowling Green to Hazard, community leaders and law enforcement officials have posted photos of road conditions and flood damage on social media alongside warnings to residents statewide to avoid travel if possible.
item.dc:creator: Corey Elam, NewsNation

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Trump administration reforms the EPA, cleanups of America's most toxic sites are uncertain
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just over a mile from where Patricia Flores has lived for almost 20 years, a battery smelter plant spewed toxic elements into the environment for nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eastern U.S. braces for flooding while fire-damaged California city now faces mudslides
item.description: SIERRA MADRE, Calif. (AP) — Much of the Eastern U.S. braced for a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather on Saturday, with thunderstorms and melting snow combining to pound the Ohio and Tennessee valleys. The lower Mississippi valley was facing the threat of strong tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender Indiana student planned 'Parkland part two' high school shooting: Docs
item.description: A transgender high school student in Indiana is accused of planning to kill masses of students on Valentine's Day in what she called 'Parkland part two.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Tigers, zebras and other stuffed animals will get new homes after health concerns kept them hidden
item.description: Crocodiles, monkeys, tigers, zebras and dozens of other taxidermy animals will move to new homes after concerns about arsenic exposure forced the closure of the South Dakota museum where they had been displayed for decades.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump rolls back CFPB, USAID and Department of Education: Where things stand
item.description: President Trump supercharged his overhaul of government agencies this week that included a fresh round of a widespread firings.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: 2 people shot in Philadelphia as city celebrates Eagles victory
item.description: Two people were shot in Philadelphia Friday afternoon as the city held its Eagles Super Bowl Parade, Philadelphia Police said. The shooting happened outside of the parade footprint, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much, according to AP-NORC polling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending — but polling also shows that many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs such as Social Security.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: A few House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges who have blocked Trump administration actions, including those of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), backing up tech billionaire Elon Musk’s call for a "wave of judicial impeachments.” Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy will kick off his bid for Ohio governor in Cincinnati on Feb. 24
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Vivek Ramaswamy is preparing to launch his 2026 bid for Ohio governor on Feb. 24 in his native Cincinnati, with ensuing announcements held over two days in the Columbus, Toledo and Cleveland areas, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order stripping funds from schools requiring COVID-19 vaccines
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Friday to defund schools and other education agencies that require COVID-19 vaccines for students and staff. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump border czar and NYC mayor interview with 'Fox & Friends' on immigration commitments
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reestablishing an immigration office at New York's notorious Rikers Island jail is just the first step in a more complex agreement President Donald Trump's administration has reached with Democratic Mayor Eric Adams, the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico continues to dig for border tunnels, US watches
item.description: Mexican soldiers and police have resumed their search for illegal cross border tunnels between Juarez and El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE gets mixed reviews from conservatives who have long wanted major budget cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Conservatives who have spent decades promoting deep cuts to government are giving mixed reviews to the Department of Government Efficiency, the Elon Musk-led project to slash the federal workforce and reduce or end disfavored programs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Fox News embeds with ICE pursuing targets, including illegal migrant wanted for sexual assault conviction
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement's (ICE) Enforcement and Removal Operations in Houston gave Fox News an inside look at day-to-day operations, as officers make detentions and target illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Driver in Utah escapes car before train smashes into vehicle
item.description: Video appears to show that the SUV may have been pushed onto the tracks by another vehicle, just as the crossing gates were beginning to lower.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Private border wall in South Texas still standing despite fraud by group fundraisers
item.description: Border Report revisited a privately built 3.5-mile segment of border wall south of Mission, Texas, after Steve Bannon pleaded guilty for fraud in a crowdsourcing scheme that raised millions of dollars for the project but paid very little for the building of the wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4
